title: torilis arvensis subsp. divaricata
intro: Torilis arvensis subsp. divaricata, also known as Divaricate Hedge Parsley, is a subspecies of the Torilis arvensis plant, a type of wildflower that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Divaricate Hedge Parsley include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support respiratory health. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
